fields.description:
## Problem
The scc LOC/size analyzer for the code_health lens: produces the `loc` payload of `AnalyzerResult [rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]` from `scc --format json`, fails closed (Unavailable) when scc is unavailable, and never fabricates zeros.

## Approach & rationale (G6/T1)
scc's per-file LOC JSON is ALREADY parsed in-repo: `_scc_files(data) -> list[dict]` at `src/rebar/grounding/engine_b_backends.py:412` returns entries carrying `Code` (int LOC) and `Location` (path), consumed by `_metric_invoke` (:376) via `harness.run_tool([binary,"--format","json",repo_root])`. REUSE it: extract `_scc_files` VERBATIM into `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/_scc.py` and re-import it in engine_b_backends (one definition, no drift). Rationale: single parser, zero schema drift; this is the established in-repo scc pattern, not a novel architecture (T1). New analyzers live in `analyzers/` (NOT the quarantined `adapters/`).

## Concrete contracts (single source of truth — 0734 reads these)
- SHARED PARSER: `analyzers/_scc.py: _scc_files(data: Any) -> list[dict[str, Any]]` (entries `{"Code": int, "Location": str}`), extracted verbatim; engine_b_backends imports it from here.
- LOC PAYLOAD produced by this adapter: `AnalyzerResult.loc = {"files": {<relpath:str>: <loc:int>}, "max_loc": <int>}` where `files` is built from `_scc_files` and `max_loc = max(files.values(), default=0)`.
- ENTRY POINT: `scc_loc.analyze(repo_root, *, run=<runner>=harness.run_tool) -> AnalyzerResult | Unavailable`.
- FAIL-CLOSED: a missing binary, non-zero exit, unparseable JSON, or an abstained run -> `Unavailable(reason, _ACCRUING_SINCE)` (mirrors `_metric_invoke` failing open to abstain). A SUCCESSFUL empty result (`[]`, e.g. all-ignored tree) -> `AnalyzerResult(loc={"files": {}, "max_loc": 0})` (measured "no files", NOT Unavailable).

## Grounding (E4 — verified via Read)
- CONFIRMED engine_b_backends.py:376 `harness.run_tool([binary,"--format","json",str(repo_root)])`; :412 `_scc_files(data)->list[dict]`; :410-420 read `entry.get("Code")` / `entry.get("Location")`.
- `AnalyzerResult` / `Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` / `resolve_analyzer(lang)` from prerequisite `[rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]`; `[metrics]` extra from `[rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed]`; `[code_health]` config from `[rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb]` (all depends_on edges recorded).

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] `_scc_files` is defined once in `analyzers/_scc.py`; engine_b_backends imports it from there. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_single_parser` asserting `engine_b_backends._scc_files.__module__ == "rebar.metrics.analyzers._scc"`.
- [ ] on a fixture (files 100 & 850 LOC) `scc_loc.analyze` returns `AnalyzerResult.loc == {"files": {...}, "max_loc": 850}`. PROVE (integration, skipif scc absent): `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_live -m integration`.
- [ ] scc unavailable (runner abstained / non-zero / missing) -> `Unavailable(reason, _ACCRUING_SINCE)`; no crash, no zeros. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_unavailable` (monkeypatched run returns an abstained/failed result).
- [ ] successful empty output -> `AnalyzerResult(loc={"files":{},"max_loc":0})`, not Unavailable. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py::test_empty_tree`.

## Dependencies: 3b30, 9597, e7a0 (recorded depends_on).
## Files likely touched
- src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/_scc.py (new; extracted `_scc_files`)
- src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/scc_loc.py (new; `analyze()` builds AnalyzerResult.loc)
- src/rebar/grounding/engine_b_backends.py (import `_scc_files` from the shared module)
- tests/unit/metrics/test_scc_loc.py (new)
## Estimated scope: M
